content: We can refactor curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (from ICM/RND curious agent research) into a hierarchical memory pruning gate for state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems, where only unexpected, high-curiosity environmental experiences get preserved in long-term agent memory instead of being filtered by static prediction error thresholds. This creates an autonomous self-improving memory loop that aligns memory storage directly with the agent's active learning goals.
